Enterprise Kubernetes stability comes down to the basics. 🏗️
🔹 Always define CPU/Memory requests & limits to stop noisy neighbors.
🔹 Enforce least privilege with strict RBAC.
🔹 Use liveness & readiness probes for smooth traffic routing.
#Kubernetes #CloudArchitecture #GCP

GCP costs getting out of hand? Enterprise optimization isn't just about scaling down. 📉

✅ Use Custom Machine Types to fit workloads perfectly.
✅ Move stale data to Cloud Storage Coldline/Archive.
✅ Enable GKE cost allocation.

#GCP #CloudArchitecture #Kubernetes #OpenSource

2026年3月24日、LLMプロキシライブラリ LiteLLM の PyPI パッケージが侵害されました。 攻撃者は PyPI のメンテナアカウント（krrishdholakia）を乗っ取り、クレデンシャル窃取・Kubernetes 対象のラテラル・永続化マルウェアを含むバージョン 1.82.7 および 1.82.8 を公開しました。 本記事では公開情報をもとに、事象...

The PyPI package litellm version 1.82.8 was compromised, stealing SSH keys, cloud credentials, and Kubernetes secrets, and installing a persistent backdoor through a malicious .pth file. If you installed it, rotate all credentials immediately. More details are available at safedep.io.

Day One is in the books for #KubeConEU! 

Highlight of the day? Our own Michal Nosek speaking about secure databases at scale with our friends over at Chainguard. 

Come by Booth 790 tomorrow to catch some time with our experts!

It also includes the validation steps to actually prove traffic is encrypted.

Balasubramanian Jayamoorthy put together a good walkthrough here. It's worth a read if you're considering deploying #Kubernetes where the internet isn't an option. (4/4)

The guide covers real engineering trade-offs, like why Cilium replaces Flannel and kube-proxy, how WireGuard handles encryption at the kernel level without sidecars, and the version pinning gotchas that can quietly break a cluster. (3/4)

environments means every dependency, image, and binary has to be accounted for before you even start. The article below walks through deploying a full HA K3s cluster with Cilium, #WireGuard encryption, and Rancher, all completely offline. (2/4)

https://lckhd.eu/3LmYA4

There will be situations where you need to run containers in an isolated space. Running Kubernetes in #airGapped 🧵

From sidequest to something real. Still in dev, but the bones are solid.

recent_posts.text: The KubeCon lineup this year reads like a who's who of 'we realized AI needs real infrastructure.' Multi-cluster, observability, networking — all the boring-but-critical stuff that makes AI actually work in production instead of just demos.

Velero going to CNCF sandbox is huge. Backup and DR have been the unsexy but critical gap in the K8s ecosystem. When Broadcom donates something, it usually means they're done investing — but the community might actually make it better.
